ads.ad_title: Burger Starting at ₹69 — Dark Macro Ad

style.overall_style: Dark dramatic macro food photography with bold diagonal ribbon price banner

design_language.approach: Extreme close-up food hero with diagonal price ribbon slicing across upper frame
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Diagonal price ribbon upper-center → extreme macro burger dominates 80% of frame → brand name bottom
design_language.composition: Near full-bleed food photography — burger fills almost entire frame, ribbon overlaid diagonally
design_language.depth: Very shallow depth of field — burger foreground razor sharp, background completely black bokeh
design_language.border_element: Diagonal red ribbon/banner with white and yellow typography — cuts across upper third at an angle

color_logic.color_emotion: Black background creates gallery-level drama — red ribbon injects urgency and brand identity — yellow price number is the conversion trigger against the dark field

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold condensed sans-serif — mixed weights on ribbon
typography.headline_size: Price number (₹69) is largest — ribbon text large but constrained to banner width
typography.brand_name_style: Lowercase Zomato signature font — bottom center
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Price number largest → category word large → supporting text medium → brand name base
typography.text_case: All caps on ribbon, lowercase brand

text_placement.price_ribbon: Upper third, diagonal left-to-right angle across frame

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Full frame — extreme macro burger, slightly off-center right, filling 80%+ of frame
graphics_and_image_placement.background: Pure black bokeh — no surface visible
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Extreme macro close-up, very shallow DoF, dramatic side lighting making textures pop

message_delivery.primary_message: Shockingly low entry price point for burgers
message_delivery.strategy: Price shock + appetite overwhelm — the burger's scale and detail triggers hunger before the price seals the deal
message_delivery.cta_style: Implicit — brand name at bottom is the action anchor
message_delivery.urgency_level: Very high — specific low price with free delivery on first order stacked

style.overall_style: Warm pastel lifestyle food photography with clean typographic price layout

design_language.approach: Clean pastel background with hand-held product photo and top-aligned text block
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Brand name top → supporting text → large price → hand-held burger center-lower
design_language.composition: Top zone text block on clean background → lower zone hand-held food photography
design_language.depth: Flat pastel background — product photography layered above with natural shadow
design_language.border_element: Decorative dashes flanking supporting text line — subtle editorial touch

accent_colors: Zomato red (price number — single accent color pop)
Black (primary text — strong contrast on light background)
Green lettuce, red tomato, yellow cheese (food natural colors)

text_colors.price_number: Zomato red — only color break in otherwise monochrome text block

color_logic.color_emotion: Warm cream background creates comfort and approachability — single red price number creates visual tension that draws the eye immediately to the value offer

typography.headline_font_style: Bold wide sans-serif — heavy weight for price, lighter for supporting
typography.headline_size: JUST ₹69 is very large — takes up significant upper frame space
typography.supporting_text_style: Medium weight sans-serif, sentence case with decorative dashes
typography.brand_name_style: Lowercase Zomato signature — top center, small
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Price number largest → JUST large → supporting text small → brand name small
typography.text_case: Mixed — uppercase JUST, rupee symbol mixed, lowercase brand

text_placement.supporting_text: Below brand name, center-aligned with decorative dashes

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center to lower frame — hand holding burger wrapped in kraft paper, slightly angled
graphics_and_image_placement.background: Flat warm cream/peach — no texture, no gradient
graphics_and_image_placement.human_element: Partial hand visible holding product from below center
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Clean lifestyle shot — hand-held for intimacy and scale, natural warm lighting

message_delivery.primary_message: Accessible entry price for burgers — everyday affordability
message_delivery.strategy: Warm aesthetic removes fast-food stigma — positions Zomato burgers as casual everyday choice rather than guilty indulgence
message_delivery.cta_style: Implicit — brand name at top, no explicit order button
message_delivery.urgency_level: Medium — price is compelling but warm aesthetic doesn't push hard urgency

ads.ad_title: Burger ₹69 — Dark Side-Angle with Chips Ad

style.overall_style: Dark dramatic food photography with bold white text overlay and side-angle hero shot

design_language.approach: Near full-bleed food photography — text overlaid top-left, product fills right-center, brand name mid-right
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Text block top-left → burger center → chips foreground-right → Zomato brand mid-right
design_language.composition: Diagonal composition — text occupies upper-left triangle, food occupies lower-right triangle
design_language.depth: Strong depth — burger mid-ground sharp, chips extreme foreground creating layered depth, background dark blur

color_logic.primary_background: Near black with warm dark vignette — dramatic kitchen/restaurant mood
accent_colors: Golden sesame bun (warm amber — primary food color)

text_colors.headline: Pure white — bold, maximum contrast on black
text_colors.price_number: Pure white — same weight as headline

color_logic.color_emotion: All-black environment makes every food color pop with maximum saturation — white text reads cleanly against dark — golden food tones create warmth and appetite within the dramatic composition

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold wide sans-serif, all caps — very aggressive weight
typography.headline_size: Very large — GET BURGERS and ₹69 both compete for dominance
typography.brand_name_style: Lowercase Zomato signature — placed mid-right within food zone
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Headline and price equal large → brand name small
typography.text_case: All caps for headline, lowercase brand

text_placement.primary_headline: Upper left quadrant — stacked (GET BURGERS / STARTING AT / ₹69)
text_placement.brand_name: Center-right, within the food image zone

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center-right dominant — burger at eye level, slightly right of center
graphics_and_image_placement.secondary_product: Potato chips scattered in extreme foreground, lower right — adds depth and abundance
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Eye-level shot, dramatic side lighting, extreme foreground chips create cinematic depth layers

message_delivery.primary_message: Low price point for burgers — abundance and value
message_delivery.strategy: Cinematic food drama creates premium perception — the price then creates a powerful contrast between quality look and low cost
message_delivery.cta_style: Implicit — Zomato brand name embedded in food zone
message_delivery.urgency_level: High — specific low price with dramatic visual treatment

style.overall_style: Dark moody food photography with gaming lifestyle context props and witty copy

design_language.approach: Contextual lifestyle scene — food styled on gaming table with controller props, text overlaid upper and lower
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Discount text upper-center → food hero center → witty copy lower-left → brand badge lower-left
design_language.composition: Immersive scene photography — food and props fill frame, text floats in open dark zones
design_language.depth: Strong depth — game controller extreme foreground blurred, slate board with food sharp mid-ground, blurred background with more gaming props
design_language.border_element: Decorative thin horizontal lines flanking 'UP TO' text — subtle divider element

color_logic.primary_background: Very dark brown/black — moody gaming room ambiance
accent_colors: Magenta/hot pink (wrap outer layer — dominant bold accent)
Bright green and orange (paneer and vegetables inside wrap)
Blue and red game controller (blurred foreground)
color_logic.color_emotion: Dark background creates gaming-den atmosphere — the hot pink wraps are a jarring, scroll-stopping color contrast that immediately draws the eye into an otherwise dark frame

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold condensed sans-serif, all caps
typography.headline_size: 60% OFF very large — dominates upper frame
typography.witty_copy_style: Medium sans-serif, sentence case — more conversational weight
typography.brand_name_style: Lowercase Zomato on red pill badge
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: 60% OFF largest → +FREE DELIVERY large → witty copy medium → brand badge small
typography.text_case: All caps discount, sentence case witty copy, lowercase brand

text_placement.discount_headline: Upper center with decorative line dividers around UP TO
text_placement.brand_badge: Lower left, red pill with white Zomato text

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center frame — two pink wraps on dark slate board with white dipping sauce
graphics_and_image_placement.prop_elements: Game controller blurred extreme foreground (right), additional gaming items blurred background (left)
graphics_and_image_placement.background: Dark brown/black moody table surface with gaming props
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Styled lifestyle scene — food presented as part of gaming session context, selective focus on food

message_delivery.primary_message: Discount offer targeted at gaming/work-from-home audience
message_delivery.strategy: Context embedding — by placing food in a gaming scene, Zomato targets a specific lifestyle tribe and makes the food feel naturally belonging to their environment
message_delivery.cta_style: Brand badge with witty copy serves as soft CTA
message_delivery.urgency_level: High — 60% off + free delivery stacked, but delivery is softened by playful tone

style.overall_style: Bold horizontal banner ad with dark green gradient and full-bleed pizza photography

design_language.approach: Dark green upper zone for text, pizza bleeds from center upward, red Zomato branding strip at very bottom
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Discount text top-center → pizza bleeds upward filling center → red Zomato strip bottom
design_language.composition: Three-zone horizontal: dark green text header → pizza center → red brand footer strip
design_language.depth: Pizza appears to rise from below into the text zone — creates upward energy and motion
design_language.border_element: Solid Zomato red footer strip at bottom — full width brand anchor; red highlight box behind 'ON YOUR FIRST ORDER' text

color_logic.primary_background: Dark forest green gradient — upper zone (premium, Italian restaurant feel)
accent_colors: Zomato red (bottom strip and text highlight box)

text_colors.discount_headline: White — very large, maximum contrast on green

color_logic.color_emotion: Dark green creates Italian/premium food brand feel — red strip at bottom grounds the Zomato brand identity — white text pops with maximum contrast

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold wide sans-serif, all caps — very large
typography.headline_size: ₹120 OFF is the dominant text element across upper zone
typography.supporting_text_style: Bold sans-serif, smaller, inside red highlight box
typography.brand_name_style: Lowercase Zomato in white on red footer strip
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Discount amount largest → OFF large → condition text medium in box → brand name medium

text_placement.condition_text: Below discount, inside red highlight box, center-aligned
text_placement.legal_text: Right edge of red strip, very small

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center of frame — large pizza, overhead slightly angled, bleeding upward into text zone
graphics_and_image_placement.background: Dark green gradient upper, pizza fills center, red strip footer
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Overhead shot, pizza slightly angled, no visible surface — pizza appears to float upward

message_delivery.primary_message: Fixed rupee discount on first order — harder number than percentage
message_delivery.strategy: Rupee amount (₹120) feels more tangible and real than percentage — green creates Italian food credibility, red Zomato strip reinforces brand
message_delivery.urgency_level: Very high — specific rupee discount on first order

ads.ad_title: Meals Under ₹250 Wrap — Speech Bubble Ad

style.overall_style: Warm rustic food photography with speech bubble text overlay and stamp-style badge

design_language.approach: Warm wooden background food photography — text presented in speech bubble and stamp badge overlaid on image
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Speech bubble text top-left → wraps center → stamp badge overlaid center-right → brand name bottom
design_language.composition: Food centers the frame, text elements (bubble and badge) float organically over the food like physical labels
design_language.depth: Shallow depth — wooden surface visible, wraps sharp, background dark blur
design_language.border_element: Speech bubble shape for headline text (white with tail pointing down); square stamp/badge shape for free delivery callout (dark outline on white)

color_logic.primary_background: Warm dark brown wooden surface — rustic, artisanal
accent_colors: White (speech bubble background and stamp badge)

text_colors.speech_bubble_text: Black on white bubble — high contrast, clean

color_logic.color_emotion: Warm wood background creates homemade, trustworthy, artisanal feeling — white speech bubble and stamp feel like handwritten notes or physical price tags, reinforcing authenticity and value

typography.headline_font_style: Bold condensed sans-serif inside speech bubble, mixed weights
typography.headline_size: Price (₹250) is the largest text in the bubble
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Price number largest → headline text large → badge text medium → brand name small
typography.text_case: Mixed case inside bubble, all caps in badge, lowercase brand

text_placement.speech_bubble: Upper left, overlapping food — tail pointing toward food
text_placement.stamp_badge: Center-right, overlapping wrap — positioned on the food itself
text_placement.brand_name: Bottom center — in open dark zone below food

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center frame — two wraps sliced and displayed on wooden board, angled slightly
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Eye-level close shot, warm natural lighting, cross-section of wrap visible showing ingredients

message_delivery.primary_message: Affordable meal price ceiling — reassurance for budget-conscious ordering
message_delivery.strategy: Speech bubble humanizes the message — feels like a friend telling you about a deal rather than corporate advertising; stamp badge adds physical/tangible credibility
message_delivery.urgency_level: Medium — price ceiling offer without time pressure

ads.ad_title: Got Ingredients Not Time — Pizza Dark Green Ad

style.overall_style: Dark green premium food photography with witty headline and tilted discount badge

design_language.approach: Dark green background — bold witty headline upper-left, premium pizza center, tilted badge overlay center-right, CTA bottom
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Witty headline upper-left → tilted discount badge center-right → pizza center → CTA bottom
design_language.composition: Left-weighted text upper zone, centered pizza, badge creates dynamic diagonal tension
design_language.depth: Pizza on wooden board with dark surface — minimal background detail, focus entirely on product
design_language.border_element: Tilted/rotated rectangular badge for discount — diagonal angle creates dynamic energy; small diamond shapes at top corners as decorative frame elements

color_logic.primary_background: Dark forest green — premium, Italian restaurant ambiance
accent_colors: Yellow/bright (discount badge background — maximum contrast pop)
Yellow/gold (CTA text accent — ORDER ON in yellow)

text_colors.headline: White — large, bold on dark green
text_colors.cta_text: Mixed — ORDER ON in yellow, zomato in white

color_logic.color_emotion: Deep green creates sophistication and Italian food credibility — yellow badge creates jarring attention-pop against the dark green — the contrast is intentional and scroll-stopping

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold wide sans-serif, all caps — very commanding
typography.headline_size: Very large — takes up entire upper-left quadrant across 3 lines
typography.supporting_text_style: Bold condensed sans-serif on yellow badge
typography.cta_style_text: Bold sans-serif, bottom — mixed yellow and white coloring
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Headline largest → badge text large → CTA medium → brand name medium
typography.text_case: All caps headline and badge, mixed case CTA, lowercase brand

text_placement.headline: Upper left — large, stacked 3 lines
text_placement.discount_badge: Center right — tilted approximately 15 degrees, overlapping pizza zone
text_placement.decorative_diamonds: Top-left and top-right corners — small gold diamond shapes

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center frame — Margherita-style pizza on round wooden board, centered horizontally
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Slightly elevated angle, clean studio lighting, pizza styled with fresh basil for premium look

message_delivery.primary_message: Convenience positioning — Zomato as the solution for time-poor home cooks
message_delivery.strategy: Witty relatable copy creates emotional resonance first — identifies a real pain point (have ingredients, no time) — then positions Zomato as the logical solution before revealing the discount
message_delivery.cta_style: Explicit — ORDER ON zomato at bottom
message_delivery.urgency_level: High — explicit CTA with 60% off + free delivery stacked

ads.ad_title: Order Dinner Too — Pasta Dark Green Ad

style.overall_style: Dark green premium food photography with witty behavioral nudge headline and tilted badge

design_language.approach: Dark green background — bold witty headline upper-left, floating pasta plate center, tilted badge overlaid, brand bottom
design_language.visual_hierarchy: Witty headline upper-left → pasta plate floats center → tilted yellow badge center-right → brand name bottom
design_language.composition: Matching layout pattern to Ad 7 — consistent design system: upper-left headline, centered food, right-tilted badge, bottom brand
design_language.depth: Pasta on elegant white scalloped plate floating on dark green surface — clean, editorial
design_language.border_element: Same tilted rectangular yellow badge as Ad 7 — design system consistency; same decorative gold diamond corner elements

color_logic.primary_background: Dark forest green — same as Ad 7 — consistent campaign visual identity
accent_colors: Bright yellow (tilted badge — same as Ad 7)
Orange/tomato red (pasta sauce — warm food accent)

text_colors.headline: White — large and bold on dark green

color_logic.color_emotion: Identical color logic to Ad 7 — dark green premium field, yellow disruptive badge, white clean text — confirms this is a deliberate multi-ad design system/campaign

typography.headline_font_style: Extra bold wide sans-serif, all caps — same system as Ad 7
typography.headline_size: Very large — stacked upper-left, same treatment as Ad 7
typography.supporting_text_style: Same bold condensed on yellow badge
typography.font_weight_hierarchy: Identical to Ad 7 — headline largest → badge large → brand name medium
typography.text_case: All caps headline, all caps badge, lowercase brand

text_placement.headline: Upper left — large, stacked 2-3 lines with color accent on one word
text_placement.discount_badge: Center right — tilted same angle as Ad 7, overlapping pasta zone
text_placement.decorative_diamonds: Top-left and top-right corners — same gold diamond shapes as Ad 7

graphics_and_image_placement.main_product_image: Center frame — pasta in tomato sauce on white scalloped decorative plate, centered
graphics_and_image_placement.background: Dark forest green — same as Ad 7
graphics_and_image_placement.image_treatment: Slightly elevated angle, clean studio lighting, garnished with fresh basil — premium editorial styling

message_delivery.primary_message: Behavioral nudge — cross-sell dinner while already ordering groceries
message_delivery.strategy: Piggybacks on an existing user behavior (grocery ordering) to trigger an additional food order — smart cross-sell framing rather than standalone discount pitch
message_delivery.cta_style: Implicit — brand name at bottom, badge discount does the conversion work
message_delivery.urgency_level: High — 60% off + free delivery, but delivery softened by clever behavioral copy

consistent_zomato_elements: Lowercase 'zomato' logotype always present — bottom-center, top-center, or mid-frame
White as primary text color across all dark-background ads
Price or discount number always the largest typographic element
High-quality professionally styled food photography in every ad
Portrait/vertical format (7 of 8 ads) optimized for mobile Stories
Dark backgrounds dominate — black, dark maroon, dark green — to maximize food color contrast

campaign_design_systems_identified.shared_elements: Same price point (₹69), same product category (burger), three completely different visual approaches — dark macro, warm pastel lifestyle, dark cinematic side-angle — A/B/C testing different creative styles for same offer
campaign_design_systems_identified.insight: Zomato tests multiple visual aesthetics for identical offers to identify best-performing creative style per audience segment

campaign_design_systems_identified.shared_elements: Identical background color (dark forest green), identical badge style (tilted yellow rectangle), identical corner diamond decorations, identical typography system, identical layout grid — only headline copy and food product change
campaign_design_systems_identified.insight: Modular template-based design system — same layout shell swaps food product and copy to serve different food categories efficiently

cross_ad_patterns.design_language_summary: Across these 8 ads, Zomato employs three distinct visual aesthetics: (1) Dark dramatic food macro photography (black/dark backgrounds) for maximum appetite impact; (2) Warm pastel lifestyle photography for approachable everyday positioning; (3) Dark green premium editorial style for witty campaign copy. The consistent thread is always bold oversized typography for price/discount and high-quality food as the visual hero.
cross_ad_patterns.color_strategy_summary: Three background color families dominate: pure black (maximum drama/contrast), warm cream/peach (approachable/lifestyle), and dark forest green (premium/Italian/campaign). Food colors — golden bun, saffron yellow, tomato red, fresh green — are always the warm accent palette that provides appetite appeal against cool or dark backgrounds. Yellow appears as a high-contrast badge color in the green campaign system.
cross_ad_patterns.typography_strategy_summary: Consistent typographic hierarchy: price/discount number always largest element, supporting text medium, brand name always small and lowercase. Two distinct copy tones observed: (1) Direct promotional (GET BURGERS STARTING AT ₹69) and (2) Witty conversational (GOT THE INGREDIENTS, NOT THE TIME?). The witty copy ads use same font system but sentence-case mixed with bold caps for more human feel.
cross_ad_patterns.image_strategy_summary: Food photography ranges from extreme macro close-ups (Ad 1 — fills entire frame) to clean floating editorial shots (Ad 8 — pasta on white plate). All food is professionally styled. Lifestyle context props (gaming controller — Ad 4, kraft paper wrap — Ad 2, wooden boards) add environmental depth. No human faces — only partial hands where used.
badge_and_graphic_overlay_patterns: Diagonal red ribbon — price across upper frame (Ad 1)
Physical stamp/border badge for secondary offer (Ad 6)
Tilted yellow rectangle badge — repeated design system element (Ads 7 and 8)
Red highlight box around condition text (Ad 5)
Decorative gold diamond corner elements (Ads 7 and 8)
cross_ad_patterns.message_delivery_strategy: Three messaging tracks: (1) Pure price shock — specific low number drives immediate impulse (₹69, ₹120, ₹250); (2) Percentage discount — 60% OFF drives first-order acquisition; (3) Behavioral/witty copy — relatability hook first, discount second, drives brand love alongside conversion. All formats optimized for mobile scroll-stop behavior with bold visual hierarchy.
